claim: Zim-American Israeli Shipping Co., the U.S. subsidiary of the Israeli national shipping line, vacated its offices on the 16th–17th floors of WTC 1 (North Tower) approximately one week before September 11, 2001 — breaking its lease (which ran through year-end) at a reported $50,000 cost — and relocated its North American headquarters to Norfolk, Virginia.

sources.label: Crain's New York Business — “Zim-American Israeli ships itself out of NY” (Apr 9, 2001)
Virginia Economic Development Partnership — “Governor Gilmore Welcomes Zim-American Israeli Shipping Co. To Norfolk” (Oct 2001)
sources.quote: The firm completed its move to Norfolk from New York City's World Trade Center two weeks before the September 11th terrorist attacks.

sources.label: Haley Press / @strategichaley, Instagram reel DVOtv6QCTFf (Feb 26, 2026) — surfacing source

notes: Crain's article (April 2001) is the contemporaneous announcement; VEDP press release confirms the Norfolk arrival. Move timing relative to 9/11 (~1 week before) is consistently reported but I have not yet fetched and verbatim-confirmed either URL — flagged as primary-link-pending. The reel's framing implies foreknowledge; the underlying real-estate move and date are well-documented but the inference of foreknowledge is not.
